extra.reason: deferred — color recalibration needs terminal-screenshot feedback to iterate against
deferred — wyk stats deserves its own session; PresetAll rename is low-value churn

Shipped: 'C' key toggles between bd list / bd list --all (PresetAll) and drops status!=closed clause for other presets via filter.QueryWithClosed. ClosedToggler interface on BDSource + MultiBDSource. Chip strip surfaces +closed pill. Tests in internal/filter/preset_test.go + TestShowClosed_TogglesStateAndRefetches.

Shipped: 'o' opens a column-visibility overlay (modeColumns); 1-7 toggles owner, wyk, repo, branch, type, status, updated. esc/o/q closes and saves to ~/.config/wyk/ui.json via new internal/uiconfig package. ID, P, and Title remain always-on. Tests in internal/uiconfig + TestColumnsOverlay_TogglesHidesColumnFromHeader + TestColumnsOverlay_PersistsHiddenColumnsToDisk.

Shipped: 'y' copies the cursor issue's full ID via OSC 52 (works over SSH/tmux). New internal/clipboard package writes the escape sequence directly to /dev/tty so bubbletea's alt-screen capture doesn't intercept it. clipboardCopy seam in model.go lets tests swap I/O. Tests: TestYank_CopiesCursorIssueID, TestYank_EmptyListSetsStatusInstead, TestYank_FailureSurfacesError. Help overlay notes the tmux passthrough caveat.

Shipped: 'u' reopens the most-recently-closed issue from this session via new beads.Client.Reopen + Mutator.Reopen + MultiBDSource routing. Close handler captures m.lastClosed; reopen clears it (single-deep vim-style undo). Read-only sources show the standard read-only hint; empty-lastClosed shows 'nothing to undo' instead of silently failing. Tests: TestUndo_ReopensLastClosed, TestUndo_NoLastClosedShowsStatus.

Shipped: renderStatsLine appends '· N human · M mine' to the status bar's left side. Computed from m.all (no extra fetch). me wired through new Model.WithMe builder from cmd/wyk/main. 'ready' slot intentionally dropped — bd ready has blocker-aware semantics no label count can approximate, and a wrong number is worse than no number. mine slot shows even at 0 when me is set so a user can verify their identity made it through. Tests: TestRenderStatsLine_CountsHumanAndMine, TestRenderStatsLine_EmptyWhenNoSignals, TestRenderStatsLine_MineSlotShowsZeroWhenMeSet.

Shipped: new internal/watch package wraps fsnotify.Watcher with a 250ms debounce + buffered-1 channel. Watcher subscribes to .beads dir per repo (catches bd's rename-over write pattern). main wires the events channel into Model.WithFSEvents; fsEventMsg triggers immediate refetch + re-arms the wait. 10s polling timer stays as fallback. Tests: TestWatcher_EmitsDebouncedSignalOnFileWrite, TestWatcher_CoalescesRapidWrites, TestWatcher_MissingBeadsDirSkipped, TestWatcher_CloseIsIdempotent + TestFSEventMsg_DispatchesNonNilCmd + TestFSEventMsg_SuspendedOnTerminalError.

Shipped: humanBadgeAgent + humanBadgeSelf styles dropped from styles.go; responsibilityBadgeFor renders plain humanBadge('HUMAN') for every human-labeled row regardless of src. Mutator.Create signature gains assignee; BDSource/MultiBDSource propagate it; updateQuickAdd refuses dispatch when m.me=='' with a status banner naming the fix. Tests: TestHumanBadge_AlwaysReadsHUMAN (replaces TestHumanBadge_DistinguishesSource), TestQuickAdd_DispatchesCreateWithCursorRepoAndTypedTitle (now asserts assignee), TestQuickAdd_RefusesWhenOwnerUnset. Memory feedback-human-badge-and-owner records the rules for future sessions.

Shipped: gofmt -w applied across the tree (9 files: cmd/wyk/{handoff_test,update}.go, internal/beads/client_test.go, internal/filter/preset_test.go, internal/tui/{columns,keymap,model,styles}.go, internal/updater/updater_test.go). Added a gofmt step to .github/workflows/test.yml that runs 'gofmt -l .' and fails the build on any non-empty output, so drift can't reland silently. No behavior change.

Shipped: fuzzy-filter matches in the Title column are now highlighted in bold amber (fuzzyMatchStyle). recomputeVisible captures sahilm/fuzzy's MatchedIndexes per row, converts byte offsets to rune indices once, stashes in m.titleMatches keyed by Issue.ID. renderRow applies highlightRunes after trunc so off-screen matches don't generate invisible ANSI noise. Clearing the query resets titleMatches. Tests: TestByteToRuneIdxs_HandlesMultiByteRunes + TestByteToRuneIdxs_EmptyInput + TestHighlightRunes_StylesMatchedRunesOnly + TestHighlightRunes_OutOfRangeIndicesDropped + TestRecomputeVisible_PopulatesTitleMatchesOnFilter.

Shipped: 'd' opens a modeDefer textinput prompt seeded with the cursor row. Submit a defer value (+1d, +1w, tomorrow, 2026-06-15 — bd parses) → wyk dispatches Mutator.SetDefer via new beads.Client.SetDefer (bd update --defer). Empty value cancels. New: keymap.go Defer binding, model.go modeDefer + beginDefer + updateDefer + 'defer' branch in handleWriteResult; source.go BDSource.SetDefer + MultiBDSource.SetDefer routing. Tests: TestDefer_DispatchesSetDeferWithTypedValue + TestDefer_EmptyValueCancels.

Shipped: 'v' marks/unmarks the cursor row (multi-select); esc in modeList clears all marks. When marks present, c/H/d operate on every marked row via new runBulkWrite + bulkWriteMsg. Close confirm switches to 'close N marked rows? [y/N]'; H adds human to all marked (add-only, idempotent on already-flagged); d prompts 'defer N rows until ▸ ' and applies to all. Sequential dispatch (not parallel) so subprocess load matches the single-target path. Row prefix shows ✓ for marked, ▶ wins on the cursor row. Tests: TestMark_TogglesAndClearsOnEsc + TestBulkClose_DispatchesAcrossAllMarked + TestBulkFlag_AddsHumanToMarked.

Shipped: golangci-lint v2.12.2 added to CI via golangci/golangci-lint-action@v6 between Vet and Build. New .golangci.yml opts into staticcheck/errcheck/ineffassign/unused on top of the default set; test files relaxed on errcheck. Fixed 15 findings in the existing tree: 5 errcheck (unchecked Fprint/Close), 5 staticcheck (.Copy() deprecation, WriteString(Sprintf) → Fprintf, SA4006 dead assigns), 2 unused functions (statusIcon, toggleableColIDs) removed. Lint runs clean against the tree.

Shipped: 'S' flips m.sortDesc; applySort grew a reverse bool parameter that wraps the per-axis less-func. setSortKey resets sortDesc on axis change (preserving reverse across an axis switch would surprise). renderHeader arrow flips via new sortDecorate signature (label, active, natural, reverse) + flipArrow helper. No-op + status banner when S pressed with sortNone. Tests: TestSortReverse_FlipsActiveAxisDirection + TestSortReverse_NoOpWhenNoSortActive.

Shipped: new internal/filters package persists named fuzzy-filter shortcuts to ~/.config/wyk/filters.json (XDG-aware, versioned schema, ErrUnsupportedVersion sentinel). Model.WithFilterAliases wires the loaded snapshot; updateFilter's enter case expands '@name' tokens via Aliases.Lookup. Miss leaves the raw query intact AND surfaces a status banner so the user knows the alias didn't resolve. No save UI yet — user hand-edits filters.json, matching the uiconfig pattern. Tests: filters package (Load/Lookup/version sentinel) + TestFilterAlias_ExpandsAtNameToStoredQuery + TestFilterAlias_MissSurfacesStatusBanner.

Shipped (minimal): ':' opens a vim-style command palette. Built-in commands: refresh, preset <name>, sort <axis>, reverse, filter save <name>, help. Unknown commands surface the known set in a status banner. ':filter save' persists the current m.query as @name via new filters.Save (atomic write through temp file). Raw 'bd <cmd>' shelling is intentionally deferred to a follow-up (overlay UI for stdout/stderr is its own design problem). Tests: TestCommandPalette_OpensAndDispatches + TestCommandPalette_UnknownCommandSurfacesStatus + TestCommandPalette_FilterSavePersistsAlias.

Shipped: noColorRequested() checks NO_COLOR (cross-tool convention, any non-empty value) AND WYK_NO_COLOR (wyk-specific escape hatch). applyNoColor() runs at main() entry; when either is set it calls lipgloss.SetColorProfile(termenv.Ascii) so badges/chips/status all render plain text. Useful for screen readers, log capture, dumb terminals, and CI runs of 'wyk --probe'. Tests: TestNoColorRequested with 6 subtests covering both vars + the no-color.org any-non-empty-value rule (e.g. single space).

Shipped: '+' (more urgent, priority--) and '-' (less urgent, priority++) bump the cursor row via new beads.Client.SetPriority + Mutator.SetPriority + BDSource/MultiBDSource routing. clampPriority keeps the range 0-4 (bd's range); edges are silent no-ops with an 'already at Pn' status. Bulk path: when marks are present, applies the same single-step nudge to every marked row (relative, so mixed priorities stay distinguishable). Tests: TestBumpPriority_PlusBumpsMoreUrgent + _MinusBumpsLessUrgent + _ClampsAtEdges (P0/P4 subtests) + _BulkAppliesToAllMarked.

Shipped: vim keys (j/k/PgUp/PgDn/g/G/u/d/ctrl+f/ctrl+b) were already forwarded to detailVP via updateDetail's default branch — bubbles/viewport's default keymap covers them. The real gap was mouse wheel: the top-level tea.MouseMsg handler was gated on m.mode == modeList and silently dropped wheel events in modeDetail. Refactored the gate to a switch: modeList → handleMouse, modeDetail → detailVP.Update, other → drop. Test: TestDetailView_MouseWheelScrollsViewport asserts the list cursor stays put and the routing doesn't panic.

Shipped: ':bd <args>' shells the raw bd command in the cursor row's workspace and shows stdout (plus stderr-folded-into-error) in a new modeOutput read-only overlay. q/esc/enter closes. New beads.Client.RawRun for the verbatim invocation; BDSource.RawBD and MultiBDSource.RawBD route by cursor's Repo (or first sub on empty repo). rawBDInvoker is an optional interface so read-only sources can still load. Tests: TestCommandPalette_BDDispatchesAndOpensOutputOverlay + _BDEmptyArgsIsUsageError + _OutputOverlayClosesOnEsc.

Shipped: modeOutput now uses a bubbles/viewport so long bd dumps scroll inside the overlay instead of overflowing into terminal scroll (which lost the header + footer). updateOutput forwards j/k/PgUp/PgDn/g/G/d/u/ctrl+f/ctrl+b to outputVP.Update; viewOutput renders outputVP.View() and surfaces ScrollPercent in the footer when the body overflows. rawBDMsg handler primes the viewport content + GotoTop. WindowSizeMsg sizes both detailVP and outputVP. Test: TestCommandPalette_BDOutputUsesViewport asserts the viewport receives the captured body and the rendered overlay surfaces both header and body.

Shipped: 'O' opens modeAssign prompt seeded with the cursor row's current Owner so confirm/typo-fix is one keystroke. Enter dispatches Mutator.SetAssignee via new beads.Client.SetAssignee (bd update --assignee); empty value is honored as a deliberate clear (matches bd's behaviour; the create-time owner-required rule is intentionally narrower — QuickAdd only). Bulk-aware: with marks present the typed value applies to every marked row. Tests: TestAssign_DispatchesSetAssigneeWithTypedValue + _EmptyValueClearsOwner + _BulkAppliesToAllMarked.

Wontfix / already implemented. The issue description was incorrect — 'wyk init' already installs the post-commit hook by default (Step 2 in runInit at cmd/wyk/init.go:175). The -force/-chain flags govern behavior on existing hooks; -skip-bd-init / -skip-register suppress other steps; no separate flag is needed for the hook because it's the load-bearing step. Filed in error during the candidate-planning pass.

Shipped: new cmd/wyk/dashboard.go subcommand walks every registered bd workspace sequentially (matched to MultiBDSource's HUMAN-BLOCK semaphore — no concurrent fanout) via beads.Client.ListAll, counts per-repo {open, human, closed in window}, and emits a tabwriter table with a TOTAL row. -days N adjusts the window (default 7); -json variant produces a structured object with window metadata + per-repo rows. Per-repo errors are recorded on the row (so the dashboard stays partial-but-useful) and the exit code is 1 when any repo failed. Tests: TestEmitDashboardTable_ColumnsAndTotals + TestEmitDashboardJSON_RoundTrips. main.go dispatches the 'dashboard' subcommand and lists it in the top-level usage.

Shipped: viewHelp now includes a 'Status column' legend after the Notes section. Each row renders the status name using its real statusStyleFor so users see the actual color/strike treatment, plus a one-line gloss. The 'wip' entry calls out the in_progress → wip abbreviation. Test: TestHelpOverlay_IncludesStatusLegend pins section header + every status row. Note: the issue description mentioned ○/◐/●/❄/✓ icons but wyk's actual Status column uses styled text (abbrevStatus); the legend matches what wyk actually renders.

Shipped: ':filter list' opens modeOutput overlay showing every saved alias sorted alphabetically (reuses the viewport so a registry of 50+ aliases stays scrollable); empty list surfaces a status hint instead of an empty overlay. ':filter remove <name>' deletes from m.filterAliases.Aliases and persists via filters.Save (atomic write through temp file). Missing name → usage status; unknown name → no-alias status. Updated dispatchFilterCommand default error to enumerate save/list/remove. Tests: TestCommandPalette_FilterListShowsSorted + _FilterListEmptyShowsHint + _FilterRemoveDeletesAndPersists + _FilterRemoveMissingNameUsage.

Shipped: new cmd/wyk/export.go subcommand walks every registered bd workspace sequentially (matches dashboard policy — no parallel fanout) calling ListAll + Ready in sequence so a ○ would-you-kindly-nkg ● P3 tui: '?' reverse fuzzy search — vim-natural complement to '/'

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Ready: 1 issues with no active blockers

Status: ○ open  ◐ in_progress  ● blocked  ✓ closed  ❄ deferred failure doesn't swallow the issue list. Emits an indented JSON object: exportDump{exported_at, repos[{name, path, issues, ready_ids, error}]}. Per-repo errors fold into Err with prefix ('list-all: …' or 'ready: …'); a fully-broken repo gets both. Exit 1 if any repo errored. Test: TestEmitExportJSON_ShapeAndIndentation round-trips the dump and pins both happy and errored rows. main.go dispatches 'export' subcommand and lists it in the top-level usage.

Wontfix on reconsideration. The vim-natural mental model behind '?' is 'direction-of-search' (vim's n/N depend on whether the original search was / or ?). wyk's fuzzy filter is non-directional — it narrows the visible set rather than iterating between matches — so there's no n/N to invert. The proposed ']/[' inversion would change navigation behaviour in a way that's more confusing than useful: ] / [ jump between human-flagged rows, not between fuzzy hits, so 'orient toward the next match' has no analog. Filed candidate without thinking through the UX.

Shipped: 'L' opens modeLabel prompt; on submit, the cursor row toggles the label (AddLabel if absent, RemoveLabel if present) — mirrors H's single-target toggle. Bulk path (marks present) is add-only with HasLabel idempotency check, matching H's bulk behavior so a typo can't bulk-remove an unrelated label. Action strings are 'label:<name>' and 'unlabel:<name>' so handleWriteResult can render 'labeled a-1 needs-review' / 'removed needs-review from a-1' via a strings.Cut split. bulkVerbs['label'] = 'labeled'. Tests: TestLabel_AddsWhenAbsent + _RemovesWhenPresent + _BulkIsAddOnly + _ReadOnlyShowsHint.

Wontfix on investigation. bd's notes field is bare text with no per-note metadata: 'bd note <id> text' simply appends text + '\n' to a single string field; there's no created_by or created_at per note, no structured delimiter that survives multi-line notes (a single 'bd note --file' write can embed \n). Without a reliable per-note delimiter, splitting into bullets risks fragmenting a multi-line note into bogus separate entries. The issue's premise ('bd notes carry created_by + created_at') was wrong — those fields exist on the ISSUE, not per-note. Improvement would require a bd-side schema change to expose structured note metadata; until then, rendering the raw Notes string verbatim (current behavior) is correct.

Shipped: 'e' on a row → beginEdit creates a wyk-edit-*.md temp file seeded with the cursor row's description (Detailer-enriched when available), then tea.ExecProcess( <tmpfile>) suspends the TUI. On editor exit, editFinishedMsg lands → handleEditFinished reads the temp, compares with the original body, and dispatches Mutator.SetDescription only on a real change. $EDITOR fallback is 'vi'. New beads.Client.SetDescription uses --description-file so multi-line and arbitrary-character content survives unescaped. handleEditFinished has explicit branches for no-change, editor error, and target-vanished. Temp file is removed in defer regardless of outcome. Tests: TestEditFinished_DispatchesSetDescriptionOnChange + _NoChangeIsNoOp + _EditorErrorSurfaces + _CancelsWhenTargetVanishes.

Shipped: new cmd/wyk/activity.go subcommand. bd doesn't expose a workspace-wide event log; wyk derives activity from per-issue UpdatedAt — a close proxy that's still useful for 'what happened today across my projects' digests. -since <duration> (default 24h) controls the window; -json variant for tooling. Walks each repo sequentially via injectable activityClient factory (mirrors export.go), filters issues with UpdatedAt > cutoff, merges into a newest-first sorted slice. Per-repo errors fold into hadError; empty window prints '(nothing touched in the window)'. Tests: TestCollectActivity_FiltersAndSorts (in/out of window, sort order, hadError on stub failure) + TestEmitActivityTable_EmptyWindow + TestEmitActivityTable_RendersRowsInOrder.

Shipped: ':assign', ':priority <0-4>', ':label' command-palette siblings to the existing O/+-/L keys. Bare ':assign' and ':label' open the corresponding prompts (same as the keys); ':assign <owner>' and ':label <name>' short-circuit and dispatch directly. ':priority' takes an absolute value (distinct from +-'s relative bump); out-of-range / non-numeric / missing arg → usage error. Each command honors marks via runBulkWrite when present; single-target path uses the existing issueExists guard. Updated the default unknown-command status to enumerate the new siblings. Tests: TestCommandPalette_AssignWithValueDispatchesDirectly + _PriorityAbsoluteSetsValue + _PriorityOutOfRangeIsUsageError (4 subtests: 5/-1/foo/missing) + _LabelWithValueTogglesOnRow (removes-when-present / adds-when-absent subtests).

Shipped: new 'wyk help [--markdown]' subcommand. Default invocation points users at the in-TUI overlay ('press ?'); --markdown emits a generated keymap reference (per-group ## heading + key/action table) suitable for README / docs-site inclusion. New internal/tui.DocsKeymap is the structured source of truth (5 HelpGroups: Navigation, Filters & sort, Writes, Clipboard/command, Meta); tui.KeymapMarkdown renders it. cmd/wyk/help.go is a thin flag-parsing + emit wrapper. Tests: TestRunHelp_MarkdownEmitsReference (asserts headings + table preamble), TestRunHelp_NoFlagPointsAtOverlay, TestRunHelp_RejectsPositionalArg.

Shipped: 'wyk doctor' gained 5 new checks. (1) checkEditor: resolves $EDITOR (or 'vi' fallback with WARN) and confirms the binary exists on PATH; FAIL when missing. (2) checkActor: shows the audit-trail identity bd uses ($BEADS_ACTOR → git user.name → $USER), WARN when none set. (3) checkXDGPaths: per-file PASS/WARN lines for repos.json + ui.json + filters.json with the resolved XDG-aware path, WARN when not-yet-created. Wired into runDoctor's check ordering before the per-repo checks. Tests: TestCheckEditor_WarnsOnFallbackPassesOnSet (3 subtests: fallback, EDITOR=true PASS, missing-binary FAIL), TestCheckActor_PrefersBeadsActor, TestCheckActor_FallsBackToUser, TestCheckXDGPaths_PassesWhenFilePresent (seeds repos.json only, pins PASS + the two WARNs).

Shipped: modeNote now uses bubbles/textarea instead of textinput so multi-line notes (steps, context, dated entries) can be drafted directly in the TUI. enter inserts a newline; ctrl+s submits; esc cancels. Empty submission still cancels with a status banner. textarea width tracks WindowSizeMsg; chromeExtra accounts for the textarea's 6-row height (vs. 2 for single-line modes). Existing tests updated: TestNote_PromptsAndDispatchesOnCtrlS (was _OnEnter, asserts multi-line body 'line1\nline2' round-trips), TestNote_EmptyInputCancelsOnCtrlS (was _Cancels), TestNote_EnterInsertsNewlineInsteadOfSubmitting (regression: enter must NOT submit). restoreFilterPrompt's call removed from updateNote since noteArea has its own focus/blur lifecycle.

Shipped: emptyMatchCopy now returns a two-line message per preset — original content line + a recovery hint line. Hints: human → 'Tab cycles presets; r refreshes if you expected a hand-off', ready → 'Tab to cycle to human/blocked; [ to jump human-flagged', mine → 'check -me if you expected rows', blocked → 'Tab cycles', default → 'C includes closed; / fuzzy filter; Tab cycles', query miss → '/ esc to clear; Tab to cycle presets'. New user lands on a useful next keystroke instead of guessing. Test: TestEmptyMatchCopy_PresetSpecificHints with 6 subtests covering each preset + the query-miss path, pinning both the content and the recovery-hint substrings.

Shipped: CONTRIBUTING.md covers (1) dev setup (Go version, bd dependency, optional golangci-lint v2.12.2), (2) test commands (go test -race -timeout 5m, gofmt -l, golangci-lint run, single-test pattern), (3) project layout table mapping every internal subpackage to its concern, (4) commit conventions matching the existing log style (feat/fix/ci/docs prefixes + Closes bd line), (5) bd issue workflow (file before coding, src:agent label for agents), (6) before-you-submit checklist with TUI-keybinding + write-action gotchas, (7) code-review flow (roborev). Points new contributors at the right tools and the right gates without burying the lede.

Shipped: 'wyk completion <bash|zsh|fish>' emits a shell completion script to stdout. Each shell has its own emitter (emitBashCompletion / emitZshCompletion / emitFishCompletion) sharing wykSubcommands as the source of truth — adding a subcommand to main.go requires adding it to wykSubcommands too. Bash uses _wyk + complete -F; zsh uses #compdef + _arguments/_describe; fish uses __fish_wyk_no_subcommand + per-cmd complete lines. Per-subcommand flag completion intentionally out of scope (each subcommand has flag.PrintDefaults). Tests: TestRunCompletion_EmitsScriptPerShell (3 subtests, pins per-shell sentinel + a deep subcommand name like 'doctor' so a truncated emit doesn't pass), TestRunCompletion_RejectsBadArgs (missing-shell + unknown-shell, exit 64; stderr redirected).
